courseName: Turbine Aeroplane Aerodynamics Structures and Systems 1
deptName: ช่างอากาศยาน
standardRef: EASA PART 66 MODULE 11A TURBINE AEROPLANE AERODYNAMICS STRUCTURES AND SYSTEMS
learningOutcomes:
Be able to know, understand, explain and apply the Theory of Flight, Airframe
Structures General Concepts and Airframe Structures knowledge, Equipment and furnishing,
Water and waste system and have a good attitude to work neatly, be patience with work, a
habit of working carefully and safety.
objectives:
1. Be able to know, understand the Theory of Flight, Airframe Structures - General
Concepts, Airframe Structures, Equipment and furnishing, Water and waste system.
2. Be able to understand Theory of Flight, Airframe Structures General Concepts and
Airframe Structures, Equipment and furnishing, Water and waste system.
3. Have a good attitude to work neatly, be patience with work, a habit of working
carefully and safety.
4. Be able to explain the Theory of Flight, Airframe Structures, General Concepts and
Airframe Structures - Aeroplane. Structures, Equipment and furnishing, Water and waste system
in their higher education.
competencies:
1. Describe and codify knowledge about the Theory of Flight, Airframe Structures - General
Concepts and Airframe Structures Aeroplane, Equipment and furnishing, Water and waste system.
2. Apply their knowledge of the Theory of Flight, Airframe Structures General Concepts and
Airframe Structures, Equipment and furnishing, Water and waste system in higher education.
description:
To study about Theory of Flight including Aerodynamics and Flight Controls. The
Airframe Structures General Concepts Fuselage (ATA53, ATA 52, ATA 56), Wings ATA 57,
Stabilizers ATA 55 and Nacelles/Pylons ATA 54, Equipment and furnishing ATA 25 including
Emergency equipment requirements and Cabin lay-out, Water and waste system ATA38.
